title: Urban Heat Island Mitigation
description: Which green infrastructure strategies most effectively reduce urban heat islands in large metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user wants to identify evidence‑based green infrastructure interventions—such as green roofs, vertical forests, urban parks, cool pavements, and reflective surfaces—that have demonstrable impact on ambient temperature reduction in cities. Relevant documents should include peer‑reviewed studies, city‑wide monitoring reports, or policy assessments that provide quantitative temperature change data, cost‑effectiveness analyses, and recommendations for implementation in metropolitan contexts. Documents lacking measurable evidence or focusing solely on conceptual proposals are considered less relevant.

title: Urban Vertical Farming
description: How can small apartments implement vertical farming effectively?
narrative: The user is a city resident looking for guidance on vertical farming in apartments, seeking recommendations on equipment, plant choices, space optimization, and maintenance, preferring evidence‑based solutions and best practices.

title: Urban Food Waste
description: What innovative strategies have cities implemented to reduce food waste at the municipal level?
narrative: The user is a city planner seeking evidence‑based interventions, pilots, policy frameworks, technological solutions, and measurable outcomes. Relevant documents must include case studies, reports, evaluations, and guidelines from international cities that demonstrate real implementation and impact.

title: AI Medical Imaging
description: How can deep learning models be integrated into real‑time diagnostic workflows for early detection of malignant cells in histopathology images, considering regulatory compliance and data privacy?
narrative: The user is a biomedical engineer seeking actionable information on deploying AI in clinical settings, with an emphasis on practical implementation, regulatory guidance, privacy safeguards, and real‑world performance evidence.

title: Sustainable Caribbean Family Tours
description: Which Caribbean tour operators offer fully certified, family‑friendly itineraries that emphasize environmental stewardship and support local communities?
narrative: The user wants a list of Caribbean tour companies that cater to families and are committed to ecological responsibility. Relevant answers should include details on sustainability certifications (e.g., Green Council, ISO 14001), evidence of low carbon practices, waste reduction measures, and programs that benefit local communities (e.g., community‑based tourism, cultural education). The response should not include general tourism facts or services unrelated to sustainability or family suitability.}

title: Remote Work Productivity
description: What factors influence employee productivity when working remotely, and how can organizations measure and improve it?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date research on the drivers and hindrances of productivity in remote work settings, including quantitative metrics, psychological factors, and organizational practices. Relevance is judged by studies, surveys, or evidence‑based reports that address specific productivity measures and actionable strategies.

title: Remote Work Urban Impact
description: I want to understand how widespread remote work policies are influencing urban development, public transit usage, and housing markets in major metropolitan areas.
narrative: The user seeks research studies, policy analyses, and statistical evidence that discuss changes in city infrastructure, zoning, and transportation demand due to increased telecommuting. Relevant content should include peer‑reviewed articles, government reports, and data‑driven case studies from the last five years, focusing on North American, European, and Asian megacities.

title: Microplastics in Oceans
description: What are the ecological impacts of microplastics on marine ecosystems and how do they influence food webs?
narrative: The user seeks scientific studies and environmental assessments that detail how microplastics enter marine food chains, their effects on various trophic levels, and mitigation strategies, excluding general news or anecdotal reports.

title: Rural Telemedicine Impact
description: How has telemedicine adoption affected health outcomes in rural communities?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based studies that quantify changes in key health metrics—such as hospitalization rates, preventive screening rates, patient satisfaction, and cost per encounter—following the implementation of telemedicine services in rural settings. Relevant documents should be peer‑reviewed, specify the definition of 'rural', detail the telemedicine modality used, and provide before‑and‑after comparisons or control groups. Opinion pieces, non‑empirical reports, or studies focused on urban populations should be considered non‑relevant.

title: Urban Vertical Farming
description: What are the most effective strategies for integrating vertical farms within existing city infrastructure?
narrative: The user is a city planner researching innovative solutions for food security in dense urban environments. They seek detailed, evidence‑based strategies—including case studies, cost‑benefit analyses, and policy recommendations—that demonstrate successful integration of vertical farms with existing zoning, transportation, and utility systems, and are particularly interested in scalable models suitable for mid‑sized metropolitan areas.

title: Electric Vehicle Choice
description: What are the key considerations for selecting an electric vehicle that balances cost, range, and long‑term battery performance?
narrative: I am a prospective buyer looking to evaluate options based on purchase price, available financial incentives, expected range, battery warranty, and projected maintenance costs. Documents are relevant if they provide detailed information on pricing, government incentives, battery longevity, and total cost of ownership.

title: Rooftop Irrigation Solutions
description: What are the most effective, low‑water, low‑maintenance irrigation systems suitable for rooftop gardens in temperate climates?
narrative: The user is a small business installing rooftop gardens and needs strategies that conserve water, manage edges, avoid roof leakage, and integrate with existing infrastructure. Relevant documents should include system designs, case studies, performance metrics, cost estimates, and installation guidelines that address these constraints.

title: Roman Imperial Titles
description: How can I craft authentic Latin titles for fictional Roman emperors?
narrative: The user, an author creating a Roman-themed fantasy narrative, seeks guidance on appropriate Latin phrases for imperial titles, looking for historically faithful and grammatically correct options; relevance is judged by authenticity to contemporary Roman titulature, correct usage of designations such as "Augustus" and "Imperator", and documentation from credible historical sources.

title: AI in Oncology Treatments
description: What is the current state of AI‑driven personalized treatment plans in oncology?
narrative: The user is a clinical oncologist interested in integrating machine learning into therapy decision‑making. They seek peer‑reviewed studies, FDA‑approved AI tools, predictive model performance metrics, and evidence of cost‑effectiveness in real‑world settings. Relevant documents should provide detailed methodology, validation data, regulatory status, and case‑study outcomes that demonstrate how AI has improved treatment selection, response prediction, or patient stratification.

title: EU Digital Health Privacy
description: What are the regulations and guidelines that protect the privacy of digital health records in the European Union?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive understanding of the legal framework governing privacy in digital health contexts within the EU. Results should include core legislation such as the GDPR and the proposed ePrivacy Regulation, relevant health‑specific directives, national implementation guidelines, and landmark case law. Practical guidance from regulators and scholarly analysis on technical safeguards, consent mechanisms, and cross‑border data flows are also relevant. In short, the user wants authoritative, policy‑focused documents that explain how EU law protects digital health information.

title: Urban bike lane safety
description: What evidence exists on the effectiveness of citywide bike lane projects in reducing cyclist injuries?
narrative: The user seeks research that measures how protected bike lanes influence injury rates. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, before/after comparisons, cost-benefit studies or policy evaluations, and must exclude anecdotal or opinion pieces.

title: Joint Pain Management
description: What evidence‑based strategies can postmenopausal women use to manage joint pain?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date, clinically validated information on treating joint pain specifically in postmenopausal women. Relevant documents should be peer‑reviewed studies, randomized controlled trials, or systematic reviews published from 2015 onward that evaluate pharmacological (e.g., NSAIDs, hormone replacement, biologics) and non‑pharmacological interventions (e.g., exercise, dietary changes, supplements, physical therapy). Sources that discuss joint pain in general populations, pre‑menopausal women, or anecdotal blogs are not relevant.

title: Lighthouse Restoration
description: What are documented cases of 19th-century lighthouse restoration projects that prioritized the reuse of original materials?
narrative: The user is a maritime archivist investigating successful preservation strategies of coastal lighthouses. They seek case studies that detail the sourcing and application of original stones, timbers, or ironwork, including any conservation challenges and the resulting structural integrity. Relevance hinges on projects that explicitly mention using original materials, provide technical restoration methods, and include a complete project timeline.

title: Online Learning Accessibility
description: What strategies improve accessibility for students with disabilities in massive open online courses?
narrative: The user wants evidence‑based recommendations on UI/UX design, WCAG compliance, and adaptive technologies that demonstrably increase engagement and learning outcomes for learners with visual, auditory, motor, or cognitive impairments. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed research, industry guidelines, and case studies published after 2015 that report measurable improvements in accessibility metrics.

title: Boiler Replacement Subsidies
description: Where can I find up-to-date information on French energy subsidies for replacing boilers, including eligibility rules, eligible equipment, and refund amounts?
narrative: The user is planning to replace an old coal, oil or gas boiler and wants comprehensive, current information on all government and supplier‑run energy premium schemes applicable to this renovation. Relevance criteria include: the document must discuss recent subsidy programs (post‑2021), list specific eligible equipment (e.g., THPE boilers, heat pumps), provide refund amounts for both modest and non‑modest households, outline eligibility conditions (income thresholds, dwelling size, professional qualification requirements), and note any expiry dates or policy changes. Documents that only offer historical data, generic energy‑efficiency advice, or unrelated supplier offers are considered irrelevant.

title: Electric Vehicle Carbon
description: How does the carbon footprint of charging electric vehicles vary by regional energy mix, and what are the best practices to minimize emissions?
narrative: The user wants to evaluate the life‑cycle carbon emissions associated with charging electric vehicles in different geographic regions, focusing on energy‑mix data, emission factors, and strategies for reducing the carbon impact. Relevant documents should contain up‑to‑date regional electricity generation statistics, peer‑reviewed studies on EV emissions, and best‑practice guidelines for low‑carbon charging.

title: Lyre Reconstruction Methods
description: What documented reconstruction approaches exist for ancient European lyres and how do they differ across cultures?
narrative: The user seeks to analyze experimental archaeology studies that reconstruct lyres from various European contexts—Gallic, Merovingian, Norse, and others—focusing on materials, tools, cultural setting, and methodological differences. Relevant documents should detail reconstruction protocols, findings, and comparative discussions of how different cultures approached the restoration and re‑creation of these instruments.

title: Greek column styles
description: How did the Doric, Ionic, and Corinthian column styles evolve in classical Greek temples, and what architectural features distinguish them?
narrative: The user seeks detailed academic information on the evolution and distinguishing characteristics of the three classical orders in Greek temples, including comparative designs, proportions, key examples, and historical context, from scholarly articles and reputable sources.

title: Post‑COVID Remote Care
description: What are the best evidence‑based practices for delivering home‑based palliative care to patients with advanced cancer during the COVID‑19 pandemic?
narrative: The user is a palliative care clinician seeking up‑to‑date guidance on how to safely and effectively provide home‑based care for advanced cancer patients amid pandemic restrictions. Relevance is limited to empirical studies, systematic reviews, consensus statements, and practice toolkits that specifically address remote or home‑based palliative care, including telehealth technology, infection‑control protocols, patient‑reported outcomes, caregiver support, and cost‑effectiveness. Documents lacking pandemic‑specific context or not focused on advanced cancer should be considered irrelevant.

title: Climate Impact Economics
description: How do changes in climate affect the profitability and food security of smallholder farms in rural Sub‑Saharan Africa?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed research, policy briefs, and field studies that analyze the economic consequences of climate variability on smallholder agriculture in Sub‑Saharan Africa. Relevant documents must provide quantitative data (e.g., yield, cost, income) and qualitative insights on adaptation strategies, market access, and policy interventions over the last 15 years. Works focusing solely on large commercial farms, unrelated regions, or purely physiological plant responses are not considered relevant.

title: Delirium Risk Factors
description: Which factors predict postoperative delirium in major orthopedic surgery patients?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive, evidence‑based list of predictors—demographic, clinical, anesthetic, intra‑operative, and postoperative—that contribute to postoperative delirium in adults undergoing hip or knee arthroplasty. Relevant documents should cite peer‑reviewed studies, systematic reviews, or clinical guidelines that report adjusted odds or hazard ratios for these factors, and should focus specifically on major orthopedic surgery rather than other surgical specialties. Documents unrelated to delirium or orthopedic procedures should be considered irrelevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key legislative measures adopted at the national level in the past decade that have significantly influenced renewable energy deployment?
narrative: The user is an academic researcher focusing on policy analysis. They want to identify specific laws, incentives, and regulatory frameworks enacted between 2010 and 2020 that have led to measurable increases in renewable energy capacity. Relevance requires documents discussing national laws or policies, reporting their impact quantifiably, and giving time scope within that decade.

title: Sustainable Urban Transport in European Cities
description: What are the most effective policy measures adopted by European cities to promote sustainable urban transport between 2010 and 2024, and what evidence exists of their impact?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based policy comparisons across major European cities, focusing on interventions such as low‑emission zones, bike‑sharing expansion, congestion pricing, and public transit fare reductions. Relevant documents should include case studies, statistical impact reports, and academic or governmental analyses that report measurable outcomes—such as emission reductions, mode shift, ridership growth, or reductions in traffic congestion—within the specified timeframe. Articles or reports that lack empirical data, discuss non‑European contexts, or focus solely on technology without policy evaluation are considered irrelevant.

title: Climate Policy Impact
description: How do carbon tax proposals affect industrial emissions in the United States between 2020-2024?
narrative: The user is a policy analyst seeking to understand the immediate and lagged effects of enacted carbon tax policies on the emission profiles of major industrial sectors in the United States during 2020-2024. Relevance criteria include empirical studies, government reports, and peer-reviewed articles that quantify emissions changes attributable to policy, with data disaggregated by sector and year. Articles lacking clear attribution or focused on other countries are excluded.

title: Solar Panel Feasibility
description: What factors determine whether installing solar panels on a suburban home is cost‑effective and technically viable?
narrative: I want to evaluate practical, financial, and technical aspects of solar panel installation for a suburban residence, including upfront costs, local regulations, roof suitability, available incentives, and projected energy savings. Relevance requires coverage of these considerations.

title: Remote Work Family Dynamics
description: How has the shift to remote work during the COVID-19 pandemic affected family relationships, parenting responsibilities, and work‑life boundaries?
narrative: The user seeks recent scholarly and practitioner studies that discuss quantitative and qualitative evidence on changes in family dynamics due to remote work, including effects on child development, marital satisfaction, and occupational health. Relevant documents should provide peer‑reviewed articles, meta‑analyses, or case studies published from 2020 onward and include explicit measurement of work–family conflict or enrichment.

title: AI Healthcare Ethics
description: What are the ethical considerations in deploying artificial intelligence systems for patient diagnosis and treatment within modern hospitals?
narrative: The user seeks detailed discussions on potential ethical dilemmas, bias mitigation strategies, regulatory frameworks, and stakeholder responsibilities associated with AI‑powered clinical decision support tools. Relevant documents must explicitly address healthcare ethics, AI algorithms used in diagnosis or treatment planning, legal and policy aspects, and case studies highlighting real‑world applications.

title: Benabar Festival Presence
description: Which French‑pop festivals featured Benabar as a performer from 2018 to 2022 and who did he collaborate with?
narrative: The user wants to explore Benabar’s festival appearances over five years, specifically the festival names, dates, venues, and any joint performances or collaborations with other artists. Relevant documents should include reliable festival line‑ups, official programs, or reputable news reports that mention Benabar’s participation and co‑acts within the specified window.

title: Kodak Disc film
description: I want to learn about Kodak’s Disc film format – its specifications, history, and how it compares to other 35mm film formats.
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive technical and historical information on Kodak's Disc film, including the film’s format dimensions, shutter specifications, compatibility with camera models, production timeline, marketing strategy, and user community experiences. Relevance should include articles, scholarly resources, manufacturer documentation, and credible hobbyist accounts that directly address these aspects.

title: Remote Work Impact
description: What long-term effects does remote work have on employee mental health and productivity?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based research on how remote work influences workers’ psychological well‑being and work output over time. Relevant documents should include peer‑reviewed longitudinal studies, meta‑analyses, and systematic reviews that quantify outcomes such as stress, burnout, job satisfaction, and performance metrics. Excerpts that are anecdotal, opinion pieces, or lack methodological rigor should be excluded.

title: Urban Air Quality
description: What are the most effective technologies and data sources for monitoring air quality in large cities?
narrative: The user seeks recent, evidence‑based studies, technical reports, and pilot project case studies that evaluate sensor networks, satellite observations, and community‑driven monitoring systems for urban air quality. Relevance requires documents providing comparative accuracy analysis, cost‑benefit assessments, data integration methods, and policy implications.

title: Sustainable Leather Production
description: What are the latest technologies for reducing water usage in cattle hide tanning processes?
narrative: The user, a procurement manager for a fashion firm seeking to ensure its supply chain meets ESG criteria, wants to identify technologies and best practices that lower water consumption in leather tanning. Relevant documents should detail technical methods, case studies, certifications, and quantified water savings, and exclude generic marketing or unrelated tanning processes.

title: Regenerative Agriculture Adoption in Sub-Saharan Africa
description: What are the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of implementing regenerative agriculture practices in Sub‑Saharan African rural communities?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based studies, policy briefs and case reports published after 2015 that quantify benefits such as soil carbon sequestration, water retention, crop yields, and livelihoods. Relevance is judged by geographic fit (countries in Sub‑Saharan Africa), focus on regenerative approaches (cover cropping, no‑till, agroforestry), and inclusion of socioeconomic assessment. Documents purely discussing conventional farming or unrelated regions are considered irrelevant.

title: Urban Green Impact
description: What measurable effects do urban green spaces have on local air quality and community health?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed studies and official reports that quantify how parks and green roofs reduce air pollutants and improve mental and physical health outcomes in city residents. Relevance should include empirical data, statistical analyses, and case studies, while excluding anecdotal blogs, non‑scientific commentary, or unrelated environmental topics.

title: Sustainable Supply Chains
description: How can firms design and monitor low‑carbon supply chains that remain resilient to geopolitical and climate disruptions?
narrative: The user seeks actionable best practices, frameworks, and case studies that combine circular economy principles, ESG metrics, risk‑management tools, and carbon‑accounting methods to build resilient, low‑emission supply chains. Relevance requires coverage of governance structures, technology solutions, stakeholder coordination, and measurable indicators that address both sustainability and resilience.

title: Urban Planning Remote Work
description: How has the rise of remote work influenced urban transportation and housing development in major metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user seeks studies, data, and analyses that link remote work trends to changes in public transit usage, zoning decisions, and residential property markets, prioritizing recent (past 10 years) evidence and case studies from North American cities.

title: European Lamp History
description: How did European oil lamp manufacturers evolve from the 19th century to present, balancing aesthetic, technological, and safety considerations?
narrative: The user seeks a scholarly overview of the progression in oil lamp design, focusing on European producers, their design innovations, materials, safety regulations, and cultural impacts. Relevant documents should provide historical analyses, technical reports, or academic sources that discuss these aspects in depth rather than simple product catalog entries.

title: Sustainable Travel Options
description: Which eco-friendly transportation choices are available for traveling between major French cities, and how do they compare in cost, convenience, and environmental impact?
narrative: The user wants a detailed comparison of sustainable travel methods (high‑speed rail, shared electric cars, bike rentals, and regional electric buses) that includes price ranges, schedules, carbon emissions, and user ratings. Relevance requires documents that provide side‑by‑side data and user experiences rather than generic marketing claims.

title: Sustainable Packaging
description: What are the latest innovations in sustainable packaging materials suitable for food products?
narrative: The user seeks recent academic and industry sources that detail environmentally friendly packaging options for food items, including data on material performance, regulatory compliance, lifecycle analysis, and scalability.

title: Urban Composting Systems
description: What are effective composting methods for urban rooftop gardens in temperate climates?
narrative: The user seeks guidance on selecting, installing, and maintaining composting setups that maximize nutrient recovery while complying with city regulations. Relevant information includes case studies, technical specifications, volume calculations, cost analyses, and maintenance schedules for small‑scale composters suitable for rooftop use.
